questions.text: Are you interested in the sauna experience?

options.label: Yes, that's part of the appeal

questions.text: How do you feel about crowds?

options.label: Bring it on — that's part of the experience

questions.text: Architectural / historical context — how much does it matter?

options.label: A lot — I'd visit just for the building
Don't care, just want to swim

pools.name: Stadtbad Neukölln
pools.tagline: 1914 Roman bath. Most beautiful public pool in Berlin.
pools.transit: U7 to Rathaus Neukölln — 30 min from Alex

pools.name: Stadtbad Charlottenburg Alte Halle
pools.tagline: Berlin's oldest pool (1898). Quieter, gorgeous, real history.
pools.transit: U7 to Wilmersdorfer Straße — 30 min from Alex

pools.name: SSE Europasportpark
pools.tagline: 50m Olympic-spec lap pool. Where Berlin swimmers actually train.
pools.transit: S41/S42 to Landsberger Allee — 25 min from Alex

pools.name: Stadtbad Tiergarten
pools.tagline: Modern, central, no-fuss. Perfect for a quick post-sightseeing swim.
pools.transit: U9 to Turmstraße — 20 min from Alex

pools.name: Stadtbad Mitte
pools.tagline: Closest to the centre. Family-friendly, smaller, beginner-oriented.

pools.name: Sommerbad Kreuzberg (Prinzenbad)
pools.tagline: The legendary Kreuzberg outdoor pool. Iconic Berlin atmosphere.
pools.transit: U7 to Prinzenstraße — 25 min from Alex

pools.name: Sommerbad Olympiastadion
pools.tagline: 1936 Olympic pool with the original starting blocks. Historically loaded.
pools.transit: U2 to Olympia-Stadion — 35 min from Alex

pools.name: Sommerbad Pankow
pools.tagline: Family-friendly East Berlin local. Less crowded than Prinzenbad.

pools.name: Strandbad Wannsee (with sauna)
pools.tagline: 1920s lido + summer sauna program. Sweat, plunge into the lake, repeat.
pools.transit: S7 to Nikolassee — 35 min from Mitte

pools.name: Strandbad Plötzensee
pools.tagline: Closest pool/lake hybrid to Mitte. Sandy beach + pool infrastructure.
pools.transit: U9 to Amrumer Straße — 15 min from Alex
